title: Kitten Umbilical Infection: Why Pus is a Medical Emergency
meta_desc: Pus at your kitten's umbilical stump is a serious sign of omphalitis. Learn why this requires immediate veterinary care and how to triage the issue.
content_html:
What is an Umbilical Stump Infection?

Pus oozing from a kitten's umbilical area is a clinical sign of omphalitis, an infection of the navel. In newborn kittens, the umbilical stump is a direct pathway for bacteria to enter the body. Because their immune systems are not yet fully developed, a localized infection can quickly become life-threatening.

Urgency Level: High

This condition is a high-priority medical emergency. If you see yellow, green, or white discharge, or if the area is red and swollen, your kitten needs to see a veterinarian immediately. Without professional treatment and appropriate antibiotics, the infection can enter the bloodstream, a condition known as sepsis, which is frequently fatal for young animals.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

Taking a clear, close-up photograph of the umbilical area can be vital for your veterinarian. A photo allows the medical team to visually assess the color and consistency of the discharge and the degree of inflammation before you even arrive. This helps the clinic prioritize your kitten's case and prepare the necessary diagnostic tools and medications in advance.

faqs.question: Can I treat an umbilical infection at home?
faqs.answer: No. Home remedies or over-the-counter ointments are insufficient for omphalitis. Kittens require prescription systemic antibiotics to prevent the infection from spreading to their internal organs.

faqs.question: What are the other signs of a sick kitten?
faqs.answer: In addition to pus, look for lethargy, a refusal to nurse, constant crying, a bloated abdomen, or the umbilical area feeling hot to the touch.

faqs.question: How did my kitten get this infection?
faqs.answer: Infections can occur if the mother cat's mouth is dirty when she severs the cord, if the environment is unsanitary, or if the cord was cut with non-sterile instruments.
